# TS9008

**Category:** Typeclass Resolution · **Severity:** Error

## Message

```
summon type argument must be a type reference like Show<Point>
```

## Explanation

summon<T>() expects T to be a typeclass applied to a type.

Correct:
  summon<Show<Point>>()
  summon<Eq<User>>()

Incorrect:
  summon<Point>()       // Point is not a typeclass
  summon<Show>()        // Missing the type argument
  summon<string>()      // Primitive, not a typeclass

<!-- prettier-ignore-end -->
<!-- generated:end -->

## Example

```typescript
import { summon } from "@typesugar/typeclass";

/** @derive(Eq) */
interface Point {
  x: number;
  y: number;
}

const eq = summon<Point>();
// error: TS9008 — summon type argument must be a type reference like Show<Point>
```

## How to fix

The type argument names what you want an instance **of** — a typeclass applied to a type — not the type itself:

```typescript
import { summon } from "@typesugar/typeclass";
import type { Eq } from "@typesugar/std";

/** @derive(Eq) */
interface Point {
  x: number;
  y: number;
}

const eq = summon<Eq<Point>>();
eq.equals({ x: 1, y: 2 }, { x: 1, y: 2 }); // true
```

`summon<Show>()` (missing the type argument) and `summon<string>()` (a primitive, not a typeclass) fail the same way. If the typeclass name is right but unregistered, you'll get [TS9007](./TS9007.md) instead.
